truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 correctly identified two command injection vulnerabilities (SC-V3-004 and SC-V3-013) with exact matching evidence and severity. All other true vulnerabilities were missed. No false positives were found in the audited answer.

Items.Key: command injection via admin console exec command|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|arbitrary code execution on the host system by passing malicious shell commands through the admin console.|user enters an admin command starting with 'exec' followed by untrusted input.|replace system() calls with a strict whitelist of allowed commands or parse arguments safely without invoking the shell.

command injection in emergency audit logging|os command injection|cwe-78|high|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|system(command.c_str());|arbitrary code execution via shell injection if the audit path or environment variables are controlled.|controlled environment variables (e.g., tmpdir) or malicious audit path construction.|use direct file i/o apis (e.g., fopen/fwrite) for logging instead of system() to prevent shell injection.
